field_reference.top_level_array: Each element is one leak detector (A2D stack) on the platform.

detector_object.Name: string, required. Human-readable detector id.
detector_object.NumChnl: number, required. Channel count for this detector (1-based channel indices under leakage/<i>/).
detector_object.ChnlNames: array of strings, optional. If set, written as channel_name file inside leakage/<i>/<j>/.
detector_object.Device: array of device entries. Default BOM-alternative mode: mutually-exclusive populations, first probed I2C match drives all NumChnl channels. Per-channel device-map mode (auto-selected when entry count == NumChnl and all Bus/Address pairs are distinct): entry k is the sensor for logical channel k, each configured and linked independently.

device_object.DeviceType: string, required. e.g. MAX1363, ADS1015, ADS7924 — maps to driver / register logic.
device_object.Address: string, required. Hex address; alternatives may share one address when BOMs are mutually exclusive (list MAX1363 before ADS1015).
device_object.Channels: array of {Id, Type}, optional (preferred schema). One entry per logical channel: Id = 1-based hardware A2D input (AINx = Id-1), Type = channel kind (rop / flex / embedded) selecting the matching Types[] threshold set. A multi-entry Channels[] means a single present chip serves all NumChnl channels (forces BOM-alternative mode) and names each runtime channel directory by its mapped Id (e.g. 1/ and 4/). Drives the input symlink (in_voltage<Id-1>_raw) and ADS1015 MUX / MAX1363 scan programming. Replaces the legacy ChannelId + device-level Type/threshold fields.
device_object.Types: array of {Type, thresholds...}, optional (preferred schema). One entry per possible channel kind (populate all kinds even if unused: rop, flex, embedded). Each entry carries that type's thresholds — WarningMin, CriticalMin, WarningMax, CriticalMax, and (ADS1015/ADS7924) NormalMin, NormalMax. A channel's thresholds come from the Types[] entry whose Type matches Channels[].Type, so two channels of different types on one device get different bounds.
device_object.ChannelId: number OR array, optional (legacy; use Channels instead). 1-based hardware A2D input(s) wired to this device. Scalar (e.g. 4) = one input for this Device entry (per-channel device-map mode). Array (e.g. [1,4]) = one input per logical channel. Defaults to the logical channel index. Drives the input symlink (in_voltage<ChannelId-1>_raw) and ADS1015 MUX / MAX1363 scan programming.
device_object.Probe: boolean, optional. If true, bind kernel driver (new_device) before register writes.
device_object.Type: string, optional (legacy; use Channels[].Type + Types[] instead). Device-wide sensor kind label (e.g. rop, flex). Published as runtime .../<j>/type when no Channels[] is present.
device_object.CfgRegVal: string, optional. Space-separated byte values for CfgReg. ADS1015: first byte is config high (MUX — with a scalar ChannelId it selects the input 0xc2/0xd2/0xe2/0xf2 for inputs 1–4; with an array ChannelId it is a placeholder and the script programs each mapped input), second byte is config low (mode/comparator). MAX1363: platform monitor burst; byte0 = scan-to-CS (0x01/0x03/0x05/0x07 for inputs 1–4), first 0x1x byte = monitor channel tag (0x11/0x13/0x15/0x1f). Script patches these from ChannelId (first mapped input for an array).
device_object.LoThreshReg: string, optional. Low threshold register address.
device_object.LoThreshRegVal: string, optional. Bytes for low threshold (big-endian); scaled to min.
device_object.HiThreshReg: string, optional. High threshold register address.
device_object.HiThreshRegVal: string, optional. Bytes for high threshold; scaled to max.
device_object.NormalMin: number, optional. Direct runtime .../<j>/min when LoThreshRegVal is absent. Preferred location: inside the matching Types[] entry (per channel type); a device-level value is the legacy fallback.
device_object.NormalMax: number, optional. Direct runtime .../<j>/max when HiThreshRegVal is absent. Preferred location: inside the matching Types[] entry; device-level is the legacy fallback.
device_object.Scale: number, optional. Multiplier from raw/counts to engineering units (min/max). Stays at the device level (one ADC scale for all channels).
device_object.WarningMin: number, optional. User low-side warning bound (engineering units). Runtime: .../<j>/lwarn; also fills min if NormalMin absent. Preferred location: inside the matching Types[] entry; device-level is the legacy fallback.
device_object.CriticalMin: number, optional. User low-side critical bound (engineering units). Runtime: .../<j>/lcrit. Preferred location: Types[] entry; device-level is legacy.
device_object.WarningMax: number, optional. User high-side warning bound. Runtime: .../<j>/warn; also fills max if NormalMax absent. Preferred location: Types[] entry; device-level is legacy.
device_object.CriticalMax: number, optional. User high-side critical bound. Runtime: .../<j>/crit. Preferred location: Types[] entry; device-level is legacy.
device_object.Ads7924SoftReset: boolean, optional. Default true: write 0xaa to RESET before programming (raw I2C only).
device_object.Ads7924IntConfig: string, optional. Hex byte INTCONFIG (0x12), default 0xe0.
device_object.Ads7924SlpConfig: string, optional. Hex byte SLPCONFIG (0x13), default 0x00.
device_object.Ads7924AcqConfig: string, optional. Hex byte ACQCONFIG (0x14), default 0x00.
device_object.Ads7924PwrConfig: string, optional. Hex byte PWRCONFIG (0x15), default 0x00.
device_object.Ads7924Mode: string, optional. MODECNTRL final mode byte (reg 0x00), default 0x33 (auto-scan).
device_object.Ads7924AwakeMode: string, optional. MODECNTRL awake step before Ads7924Mode, default 0x20.
device_object.Ads7924UlChN: string, optional. Per-channel ULR override, N=1..4, hex byte.
device_object.Ads7924LlChN: string, optional. Per-channel LLR override, N=1..4, hex byte.
device_object.Ads7924_configuration_note: All Ads7924* keys are optional. When omitted, hw-management-bmc-a2d-leakage-config.sh uses defaults: soft reset on (0xaa @ RESET), INT/SLP/ACQ/PWR = 0xe0 0x00 0x00 0x00, INTCNTRL AEN = 0x0f, AWAKE 0x20 then MODE 0x33 (auto-scan). For ULR/LLR and comparator-driven INT you need Scale plus NormalMin/NormalMax (or if those are absent, WarningMin/WarningMax) on raw I2C (Probe false). Per-channel ULR/LLR overrides use Ads7924UlCh1..4 / Ads7924LlCh1..4 only when you need registers to differ from that voltage-derived window.

runtime_paths.leakage_i_j: /var/run/hw-management/leakage/<i>/<j>/ where i = detector index, j = channel number = the mapped hardware input from Channels[].Id (non-contiguous, e.g. 1 and 4). Legacy: 1..NumChnl for a scalar/absent ChannelId, or the 1-based Device[] position in per-channel device-map mode.
runtime_paths.input: symlink to the kernel IIO raw attribute for the wired hardware channel: in_voltage<Id-1>_raw (Channels[].Id; legacy in_voltage<ChannelId-1>_raw, or in_voltage<j-1>_raw when absent).
runtime_paths.lwarn: from the matching Types[].WarningMin (legacy: device-level WarningMin)
runtime_paths.lcrit: from the matching Types[].CriticalMin (legacy: device-level CriticalMin)
runtime_paths.warn: from the matching Types[].WarningMax (legacy: device-level WarningMax)
runtime_paths.crit: from the matching Types[].CriticalMax (legacy: device-level CriticalMax)
runtime_paths.min: LoThreshRegVal×Scale, else matching Types[].NormalMin, else Types[].WarningMin (legacy: device-level)
runtime_paths.max: HiThreshRegVal×Scale, else matching Types[].NormalMax, else Types[].WarningMax (legacy: device-level)

field_reference.deployment_note: Copy only the array in example_leak_detectors to /etc/hw-management-bmc-a2d-leakage-config.json (or merge with platform file). The full JSON in this file is for documentation; the BMC tool expects a top-level JSON array.

example_leak_detectors.Name: Example_Types_Channels_PerType_Thresholds
example_leak_detectors._comment: Preferred schema. Two BOM-alternative chips (MAX1363 or ADS1015) each serve both channels. Types[] lists every channel kind (rop/flex/embedded) with its own thresholds; Channels[] maps each logical channel to a hardware input Id and a Type. Here channel 1 (AIN0) is 'embedded' and channel 2 (AIN3) is 'flex', so each channel takes its thresholds from the matching Types[] entry. Runtime dirs are named by Id: leakage/<i>/1 and leakage/<i>/4.

example_leak_detectors.Name: Example_PerChannel_DeviceMap_NonSequential
example_leak_detectors._comment: Per-channel device-map mode: 2 distinct devices == NumChnl, so Device[0] -> channel 1, Device[1] -> channel 2. Each sensor is wired to hardware AIN3 (ChannelId 4), so input links to in_voltage3_raw and the ADS1015 MUX byte is 0xf2.

example_leak_detectors.Name: Example_BOM_Alt_ChannelId_Array
example_leak_detectors._comment: BOM-alternative mode with an array ChannelId: MAX1363 OR ADS1015 is populated, and whichever is present serves both channels. ChannelId [1,4] maps logical channel 1 -> hardware AIN0 (in_voltage0_raw, dir 1/) and logical channel 2 -> hardware AIN3 (in_voltage3_raw, dir 4/). The array form forces BOM-alternative mode and names the runtime channel directories 1/ and 4/.
